headline: IBM (IBM) Stock Looks Below Fair Value Despite Cautious Checks
summary: International Business Machines stock has delivered a very strong 170.6% return over the past five years, yet current valuation checks send a mixed signal, with the Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) intrinsic value estimate and earnings multiples both pointing to upside while the broader scorecard looks cautious. headline: Hongli Group Inc. Provides Update on Proposed Strategic Collaboration with Sidus Energy Storage
summary: Hongli Group Inc. (Nasdaq: HLP) ("Hongli Group" or the "Company"), a cold roll formed steel profile manufacturer, today provided an update regarding the advanced battery technology and research and development of Sidus Energy Storage, Inc. ("Sidus"), a California-based battery technology company. As previously announced, Hongli Group recently entered into a strategic non-binding Memorandum of Understanding ("MOU") with Sidus to explore a potential collaboration relating to battery manufacturing.

raw.headline: Hongli Group Inc. Provides Update on Proposed Strategic Collaboration with Sidus Energy Storage

As previously announced, Hongli Group recently entered into a strategic non-binding Memorandum of Understanding ("MOU") with Sidus to explore a potential collaboration relating to battery manufacturing.

headline: IBM Launches Compact z17 and LinuxONE Systems to Address Data Center Space and Cost Constraints
summary: IBM (NYSE: IBM) today announces new IBM z17 and IBM LinuxONE 5 configurations, marking the first time IBM is offering rack mount alongside single frame systems across its full Z and LinuxONE portfolio. The expanded IBM z17 and LinuxONE 5 portfolios now offer a wide range of deployment options, engineered with the same flagship performance, security, and ecosystem standards. headline: Chips Get Their Confidence Back, the Dow Cracks 53,000, and Trump Moonlights as a Dell Salesman
summary: Chip and memory stocks broadly rebounded Monday after last week's selloff, helping the Dow close above 53,000 for the first time as Microsoft's latest round of job cuts failed to reassure investors. Analyst upgrades lifted AMD and ASML, President Trump publicly endorsed Dell, a bitcoin miner signed a multibillion-dollar AI leasing deal, and auto-parts retailers sold off hard while EV makers rallied.
